sop:
# Normal Mode SOP

## Overview

Normal mode is the default operating mode. Agents work at a steady pace on assigned tasks.

## Priorities

1. Complete assigned tasks
2. Claim unassigned tasks that match your skills
3. Create new tasks if you identify gaps
4. Coordinate with teammates via chat_inbox
5. Report progress to Alice (Lead Coordinator)

## Work Cycle

- Check inbox first — handle messages before starting new work
- Pick highest-priority open task assigned to you
- Work on it until complete or blocked
- Mark tasks done via API when finished
- Write status updates to status.md

## Communication

- DM teammates via their chat_inbox/ folder
- Post team updates to public/team_channel/
- Escalate blockers to alice or the CEO

## Quality

- Code must be runnable (test before marking done)
- Feature code goes in backend/, strategies/, lib/ — not agent output/
- Every task must have a concrete artifact with a run command

## Knowledge Sharing — Culture & Consensus

The team maintains a shared knowledge base at `public/consensus.md`. **You should read it every fresh session** (it appears in your context under "Team Culture & Consensus").

**When to WRITE a culture entry:**
- You discover a decision that affects the whole team (strategy change, security requirement, architecture choice)
- You learn something that would prevent a teammate from making a mistake
- You complete a significant task and want to record the outcome as a norm

**How to post:**
```bash
curl -X POST http://localhost:3199/api/consensus/entry \
  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
  -d '{"type":"culture","content":"What you learned","section":"Category"}'
```
Types: `culture` (norms/learnings), `decision` (explicit choices), `group` (team agreements), `authority` (who owns what)

**Also use your `knowledge/` folder** for agent-specific notes you want to persist across sessions:
- Write important findings to `agents/{your-name}/knowledge/{topic}.md`
- Read it at the start of fresh sessions when tackling that topic again
culture:
# Team Consensus Board

*(Managed via API — see /api/consensus)*
| 1 | culture | Paper trading mode is required before any live order submission. Use PAPER_TRADING env flag. Never submit real orders without explicit founder approval. | agent | 2026-04-03 |
| 2 | culture | mean_reversion is our primary strategy (85.7% win rate in backtests). momentum and crypto_edge are disabled — 10-11% win rates. Do not re-enable without data. | agent | 2026-04-03 |
| 3 | culture | All API endpoints must require auth via Authorization header. No open POST endpoints in production. Pattern: check Bearer token before processing. | agent | 2026-04-03 |
| 4 | culture | Confidence threshold is 0.80 minimum for signal execution. Signals below 80% confidence are filtered by SignalEngine. Do not lower this threshold without backtesting. | agent | 2026-04-03 |
| 5 | decision | Kalshi is our primary trading venue. All trading infrastructure targets the Kalshi prediction market API. Task 236 blocks live API until credentials obtained from kalshi.com. | agent | 2026-04-03 |
| 6 | group | When you learn something important about strategy, security, or architecture — post a culture entry via: curl -X POST http://localhost:3199/api/consensus/entry -H "Content-Type: application/json" -d "{\"type\":\"culture\",\"content\":\"...\",\"section\":\"...\"}" | agent | 2026-04-03 |
| 7 | culture | Sprint 2 test coverage is complete: unit tests for PnL (T286), screener (T287), win probability (T288), mean reversion (T279), and CI runner (T289) all done. Run `npm test` for full suite. 96 unit + 30 integration + 576 E2E tests. Two pre-existing integration failures in smoke_test and mia_integration are known backend issues, not runner bugs. | agent | 2026-04-03 |
| 8 | decision | Sprint 2 complete (2026-04-03). Trading system now has full test coverage + CI. Cloud deployment plan ready (ECS Fargate ~$29/mo). Mobile push notification spec done. Remaining blocker before live trading: T236 (Kalshi API credentials from Founder). Next sprint should focus on: cloud deploy execution, paper trade P&L validation, and live trading readiness. | agent | 2026-04-03 |
| 9 | decision | Sprint 3 complete. CRITICAL: mean_reversion live win rate is 18.2% vs 85.7% backtest (67pp gap). Paper PnL: -$13.73 over 19 trades. 4 trades had NULL signal_confidence. DO NOT go live until divergence is resolved. Sprint 4 is focused on root cause analysis (Grace T322), parameter tuning (Ivan T324), and paper trading automation (Bob T323). | agent | 2026-04-03 |
| 10 | decision | CORRECTION (T322, Grace): The 85.7% backtest win rate was incorrect — actual backtest win rate is 55.9% (209/374 trades). Live win rate is 18.2%, gap is 37pp not 67pp. ROOT CAUSE: live_runner.js fetchCandles() uses Math.random() for mock candle data, producing meaningless signals. Fix required: replace random mock with deterministic synthetic or real historical data before paper trade metrics are meaningful. | agent | 2026-04-03 |
| 11 | decision | Sprint 4 complete (2026-04-03). fetchCandles() bug fixed (T326): replaced Math.random() with ticker-seeded deterministic PRNG. Paper trade metrics are now reproducible and meaningful. Next step: run paper trades with fixed data and compare win rate against 55.9% backtest baseline. Ivan param tuning recommendations (T324) should be applied once initial paper trade results confirm baseline recovery. | agent | 2026-04-03 |
| 12 | decision | Sprint 5 complete (2026-04-03). NULL signal_confidence bugs fixed (T331): live_runner.js now passes confidence field in trade output, pnl_tracker.js reads trade.confidence instead of hardcoding null, SignalEngine has null guard. Paper trade validation (T327) delivered 51 trades + /api/pnl/live endpoint. Trade settlement auto-running (T330). Current win rate: 35% live vs 55.9% backtest (21pp gap). Remaining blocker for live trading: T236 (Kalshi API credentials — Founder action required). | agent | 2026-04-03 |
| 13 | culture | Divergence analysis tool delivered (agents/bob/backend/scripts/divergence_analyzer.js). Current live paper win rate: 35.0% (60 trades) vs backtest 55.9% (374 trades). Gap is statistically significant (p=0.001). Live avg trade PnL: -22.18¢ vs backtest +24.76¢. Backtest worst market: INXW-25-DEC31 (23.3% WR). Live markets differ from backtest markets — market selection bias may be a factor. Recommendation: do not go live until gap <10pp; apply param tuning (lookback 20, z=2.0) and collect 100+ paper trades. Run: node backend/scripts/divergence_analyzer.js | agent | 2026-04-03 |
| 14 | culture | Live paper trade win rate (30.4% over 69 trades) diverges significantly from mean_reversion backtest (55.9%, 374 trades). Z-score = -4.17 (p < 0.05). All 3 paper-traded markets are underwater. Do NOT go live until gap is within 5pp or root cause is identified. Real Kalshi API data (T236) and larger sample size (200+ trades) required before go/no-go decision. | agent | 2026-04-03 |
| 15 | culture | Parameter sweep analysis (agents/bob/backend/scripts/parameter_sweep.js) reveals that deterministic mock data in fetchCandles() is too simplistic for meaningful parameter tuning. All zScore thresholds 0.8-3.0 produce identical signals on the same 8 fallback markets because mock candles have artificially low variance, creating extreme z-scores every time. Lookback changes do not materially affect signal quality. Conclusion: the deterministic PRNG fix made data reproducible, but the synthetic data model itself is not realistic enough to validate strategy parameters. We need either (a) real historical Kalshi data, or (b) a more sophisticated synthetic generator with regime shifts and realistic volatility, before paper trade metrics can be trusted for live go/no-go decisions. | agent | 2026-04-03 |
| 16 | culture | Built synthetic market generator (agents/bob/backend/scripts/synthetic_market_generator.js) to replace simplistic deterministic fallback. Comparison shows legacy mock data always produced mean~85 with stddev~2.5, creating extreme z-scores (-7 to +11) and guaranteed 0.95 confidence signals. New generator uses Ornstein-Uhlenbeck with market-appropriate volatility: crypto stddev~6-8, econ stddev~2-4, creating realistic z-scores. However, SignalEngine minConfidence=0.80 combined with MeanReversionStrategy confidence=-z-/3 means only markets with -z->=2.4 generate signals. On realistic synthetic data, most markets fall below this threshold. This may explain low live signal counts. Consider lowering minConfidence to 0.65 or changing confidence formula to -z-/2.0 for better signal generation. Run: node backend/scripts/test_synthetic_vs_legacy.js | agent | 2026-04-03 |
| 17 | culture | CRITICAL FIX applied to live_runner.js fetchCandles() mock fallback (Tina). Root cause of live/backtest divergence identified: mock candles were centered on hardcoded basePrice (16/56/86) instead of current market price. This created extreme z-scores (e.g., current=64 vs mean=89.7, z=-11.2) and guaranteed 95% confidence signals on every run. FIX: fetchCandles() now accepts currentPriceHint (market.yes_mid) and centers synthetic history around actual market price. Result: means now align with current prices (e.g., current=64, mean=66.8), producing realistic z-scores. With aligned zScoreThreshold=1.5, mock fallback now correctly generates 0 signals because these markets are not actually mispriced. This confirms the previous live paper trades were artifacts of bad mock data, not real edge. Trade-off: paper trading in mock mode will produce few/no signals until API credentials provide real market history. Run: node backend/strategies/live_runner.js --execute | agent | 2026-04-03 |
| 18 | decision | Sprint 6 complete (2026-04-03). Key outcome: ALL prior paper trade win rates (18.2%, 35%, 30%) were artifacts of broken mock data (fetchCandles() used hardcoded base prices → extreme z-scores → guaranteed signals). Fix applied (Tina, T326): candles now centered on market.yes_mid. Correct behavior: 0 signals on mock data. Momentum/crypto_edge hard-disabled (T325). Dashboard win rate trend chart added (T327). Realistic OU synthetic generator built (T328). System is ready — only T236 (Kalshi API credentials) blocks meaningful paper/live trading. | agent | 2026-04-03 |
| 19 | decision | Parameter sweep (T334, Ivan) complete — 96 combinations tested on 50 markets. Recommended (Balanced): zScoreThreshold=1.2, lookback=10, confidenceThreshold=0.65 (94.4% win rate, 18 signals). Max Win Rate alt: zScoreThreshold=0.8, lookback=10, confidenceThreshold=0.75 (100% WR, 10 signals). NOTE: results based on synthetic replay data — validate against real Kalshi data (T236) before treating as authoritative. Previous default confidenceThreshold=0.80 was too strict; 0.65 recommended. | agent | 2026-04-03 |
| 20 | decision | Sprint 7 complete (2026-04-03). Delivered: replay backtest engine (T332/bob), dashboard status page with Kalshi credential banner + backtest results (T333/charlie), optimized params zScore=1.2 lookback=10 confidence=0.65 from 96-combo sweep (T334/ivan), live trading prep script + go/no-go checklist requiring >40% win rate on 10 real paper trades (T335/grace). System fully ready for T236 (Kalshi API credentials) to unlock live trading. | agent | 2026-04-03 |
| 21 | culture | Applied T334 optimized parameters across all pipeline scripts (Tina). Changes: live_runner.js CANDLE_DAYS=10 (was 30), SignalEngine minConfidence=0.65 (was 0.80), MeanReversionStrategy zScoreThreshold=1.2 (was 1.5). Same params applied to kalshi_api_validator.js and run_synthetic_paper_trade.js for consistency. E2E smoke test passes (7/7). With corrected mock data centering + optimized params, live_runner now generates 1 realistic signal per run in mock mode instead of 0 or 3+ artifacts. Ready for real Kalshi data validation (T236). | agent | 2026-04-03 |
| 22 | culture | UI audit test entry - can be ignored | agent | 2026-04-03 |
| 23 | decision | D004 STRATEGIC DIRECTION: Build Kalshi Arbitrage Engine (Wen Zhou). 4-phase pipeline: (1) Market Filtering by volume + yes/no ratio (15-30%, 70-85% targets), (2) LLM embedding-based clustering for related markets, (3) Pearson correlation detection for price pairs, (4) C++ high-frequency execution (<1ms latency). Phases 1-3 run daily, Phase 4 trades in real-time. This is the real edge — every agent decision should orient toward this. Reference: hudson-and-thames-arbitragelab Pearson approach. | agent | 2026-04-03 |

tasks.title: SPRINT 10: Phase 4 E2E Integration Testing (Full Pipeline)
tasks.description: Design + execute comprehensive E2E tests: Phases 1-4 integrated. Mock Kalshi API with realistic data. Validate: 6 pairs detect correctly, spreads trigger signals, orders route correctly, risk controls enforce, position tracking accurate, P&L correct. Output: test_suite.cpp (Catch2), integration_report.md, performance benchmarks. Success: All E2E tests pass, zero data loss, <1ms latency.

tasks.title: SPRINT 11: Production Readiness Review (Pre-Launch)
tasks.description: Final gate before go-live. Comprehensive review: (1) Code review (all phases), (2) Security audit (no secrets, auth enforced), (3) Risk audit (controls vs design), (4) Operational readiness (monitoring, runbooks), (5) Compliance (Kalshi ToS, regulatory). Output: go_no_go_checklist.md, security_audit.md, risk_audit.md, runbooks/, deployment_proc.md. Success: All gates green, Founder sign-off.

tasks.title: SPRINT 10: Phase 4 E2E Integration Testing (Full Pipeline)
tasks.description: Design and execute comprehensive E2E tests for complete Kalshi arbitrage pipeline (Phases 1-4 integrated). Test data flow: markets filtered (P1) → clustered (P2) → correlated pairs (P3) → trades executed (P4). Mock Kalshi API with realistic data. Validate: (1) all 6 arbitrage pairs detect correctly, (2) spread deviations trigger signals, (3) orders route correctly, (4) risk controls enforce limits, (5) position tracking accurate, (6) P&L calculation correct. Output: test_suite.cpp (Catch2/gtest), integration_report.md, latency benchmarks. Success: All E2E tests pass, zero data loss, <1ms latency.
tasks.notes: Sprint 10 integration testing. Input: full Phase 4 implementation from T351.

tasks.title: SPRINT 11: Production Readiness Review (Pre-Launch Checklist)
tasks.description: Final gate before go-live. Lead comprehensive production readiness review: (1) code review (Alice + Dave) — all phases reviewed + approved, (2) security audit (Heidi) — no secrets, API auth enforced, (3) risk mgmt audit (Olivia + Tina) — risk controls validated vs spec, (4) operational readiness (Liam) — monitoring + alerting configured, runbooks complete, (5) compliance — Kalshi API ToS, regulatory requirements. Output: go_no_go_checklist.md, security_audit_t354.md, risk_audit_t354.md, runbooks/, deployment_procedure.md. Success: All gates green, Founder sign-off, ready to launch.
tasks.notes: Sprint 11 production readiness. Pre-requisite: T351 complete + T352 tests pass + T353 paper trades validate.
